items.title: Government rejects all UN recommendations on LGBTQIA+ rights

children.content: The Albanese government has accepted the lowest number of recommendations made by the UN Human Rights Council in 20 years, with none protecting the LGBTQIA+ community.

gallery.altText: albanese, with his partner, waves as he walks in a parade of people holding rainbow flags

thumbnail.altText: albanese, with his partner, waves as he walks in a parade of people holding rainbow flags

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

image.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Billionaires to every day people: Inquiry exposes extent of online hate

children.content: Everyone from billionaires to politicians and pro-Palestinian Jewish activists told the royal commission of the hate they receive in phone calls and emails.

image.altText: composite image of Julie Inman Grant, steven lowy and sarah Schwartz

items.title: NSW poultry industry faces nervous wait after suspected bird flu case

children.content: Nervous times ahead for the NSW poultry industry, with one producer believing it is "only a matter of time" before commercial operations are affected by H5 bird flu.

articles.title: Suspected bird flu cases detected in NSW and WA after devastating impact overseas

children.content: A suspected positive case of H5 bird flu has been identified in New South Wales, the state agriculture minister has announced.

image.altText: NSW Bennetts Beach where the first suspected case of H5 bird flu was found

gallery.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Rethinking drinking as gen Z wakes up to joy of early rising

children.content: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.

image.altText: Crowds outside of Romano's Pub on New Years Eve in the 90s

items.title: Farmers turn restaurant food scraps into 'very valuable commodities'

children.content: While diners enjoy their seaside meals, Andrew Collins is happily outside chasing the scraps.

image.altText: A close up of two hands holding a scoop of soil rich with active worms

items.title: Veteran NSW Liberal quits frontbench amid ICAC inquiry

children.content: Veteran NSW Liberal Damien Tudehope has quit the frontbench after being called as a witness in a looming corruption watchdog probe.

image.altText: a man with glasses looking ahead

items.title: Police investigate shooting deaths of two horses in NSW alps

children.content: A police investigation is underway after the discovery of two wild horse carcasses in Kosciuszko National Park.

image.altText: a horse stands in the park

items.title: 'An insult': How AI entered Sydney's underground music scene

children.content: Local musician Aidan Sammut says he was disgusted after a live music event aimed at showcasing local artists included an artist who used AI-generated content.

gallery.altText: A man in his mid fifties performs on stage under a purple spotlight.

thumbnail.altText: A man in his mid fifties performs on stage under a purple spotlight.

items.title: Souths captain Cody Walker apologises after kicking opponent

children.content: South Sydney captain Cody Walker has said he let his teammates down after being sin-binned on Friday night for kicking an opposition player.

image.altText: Cody Walker of the Rabbitohs reacts angrily

items.title: Curnow hits front in Coleman Medal race as Swans and Crows record wins

children.content: Charlie Curnow kicks six goals as the Swans cruise to a 35-point win over the Bulldogs, while Adelaide holds off a fast-finishing West Coast in Perth.

image.altText: Two AFL players in red and white celebrate a goal together

items.title: 'Deadly' Indigenous remote artists travel to Sydney

children.content: Remote Indigenous artists from around Australia are in Sydney for the annual National Indigenous Art Fair.

gallery.altText: Woman putting up an artwork in the exhibition.
A man in a bright yellow suit stands in front of an artwork with circular shapes.
A person wearing a yellow jumper, colourful scarf and knitted hat stands in a large room with paintings in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Woman putting up an artwork in the exhibition.
A man in a bright yellow suit stands in front of an artwork with circular shapes.

items.title: Cause of wrongly-positioned aircraft control in Shellharbour crash unknown

children.content: Investigators are unable to work out why a take-off control was wrongly-positioned leading to a fatal plane crash at Shellharbour Airport last October.

image.altText: Female and male investigator looking at tarmac with wreck on it, police car in background

items.title: Jewish community frustrated by delays to social media duty of care

children.content: A mandated digital duty of care for social media platforms remains more than 18 months away, despite an online safety law review recommending its implementation in 2024.

image.altText: Ms Vandenbroek walking with a handbag over her should looking serious outside a building.

items.title: Worst start to ski season since 1957, as blizzard finally dumps snow

children.content: Following the worst start to a ski season in decades, a major snowstorm hit the NSW and Victorian Alps last night.

image.altText: Snow setting. One boy is pushing another boy on the back so he falls on his face in the snow

gallery.altText: Perisher field with snow, dark shadows of trees, on a dark winter's afternoon.
A stake with measurements and snot surrounding it to 25cm mark

A stake with measurements and snot surrounding it to 25cm mark

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: $184m budget push 'does not touch the sides' of demand, DV body says

children.content: A recent $184.1 million state budget investment is not enough of a "breakthrough" for critically outstretched domestic violence services, says Delia Donovan.

image.altText: Delia Donovan with blonde hair, a pink skirt, black blazer, white skirt, smiling while sitting.

items.title: University's chancellor quits as ICAC inquiry continues

children.content: The University of Wollongong chancellor, Michael Still, has resigned as the state's corruption watchdog continues to investigate allegations of improper governance, recruitment decisions and consultancy engagements.

image.altText: Man sitting speaking into a microphone

items.title: Victims 'thrown into uncertainty' by Christian Brothers court ruling

children.content: Lawyers argue the court's decision to grant a moratorium had thrown abuse survivors into uncertainty.

image.altText: External images of a church owned property.

items.title: State cracks down on sale of potentially deadly 'nangs'

children.content: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW  has announced reforms will come into place shortly.

image.altText: A collection of small metal bulbs.

items.title: Hearing is believing as national braille music camp celebrates 40 years

children.content: Four decades ago, 92-year-old Roma Dix co-founded a braille music camp for visually impaired children. This week they're celebrating its 40th anniversary in full song.

gallery.altText: Group of people, children and adults, holding musical instruments
Man of asian decent playing guitar

Man of asian decent playing guitar
